key_rights: The University is open to all individuals, regardless of their race, creed, or social class.
Students have the right to a fair process for addressing grievances, such as being debarred from exams or facing disciplinary action, through a special Tribunal of Arbitration.
important_provisions: Establishes the National Forensic Sciences University (NFSU) as an institution of national importance in India.
Aims to be a center of excellence for teaching, training, and research in forensic science, applied behavioral science, law, criminology, and related technologies.
Seeks to strengthen criminal justice systems by developing expertise, promoting innovation, and coordinating with government bodies.
Formed by merging the Gujarat Forensic Sciences University and the Lok Nayak Jayaprakash Narayan National Institute of Criminology and Forensic Sciences.
Designates NFSU as a 'public authority' under the Right to Information (RTI) Act, 2005, promoting transparency and accountability.
Protects the service conditions of existing employees and the academic progress of students from the merged institutions during the transition.
important_penalties: The Act does not outline any specific penalties or fines for ordinary citizens.
Disciplinary actions against students are internal university matters, handled through established redressal mechanisms.
who_can_benefit: All individuals seeking education or research in forensic science and related fields, regardless of race, creed, or social class.
Students of the University, through fair grievance redressal processes.
Ordinary citizens, by being able to request information from the University under the RTI Act, promoting transparency.
Existing employees and students from the merged institutions, through protected service conditions and academic progress.
Criminal justice systems in India, by gaining expertise, innovation, and improved investigation and prevention methods.
The nation of India, by having a center of excellence in forensic sciences and related fields.
